rationale: The citation is used in the core literature review and conceptual setup, not as a passing mention. The authors specifically summarize Ding and Zhang (2020) Experiment 3 as evidence that conditional promotions increased promoted-product sales versus no promotion, with stronger effects at higher discount levels. They use this result to define the prior pattern in the field and motivate their own boundary-condition test. Their contribution then qualifies that prior pattern by showing reversal when precondition cost is high.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Ding and Zhang (2020) as prior evidence that conditional promotions can boost sales, especially with higher discounts, in typical low-precondition settings. It refines that implication by identifying precondition cost as a key boundary condition under which high-discount conditional promotions can instead reduce total sales versus no promotion.
